text.title: pyproject.toml as the Single Source of Configuration Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the pyproject discipline and skip path.

rule.statement: pyproject.toml MUST contain a `[project]` table with PEP 621 keys (name, version, requires-python, dependencies).
rule.rationale: PEP 621 is the standard metadata location; modern tools read from here.

rule.statement: Every tool the project uses (ruff, pytest, coverage, ty/mypy, bandit, mutmut, uv) MUST be configured under its `[tool.<name>]` table in pyproject.toml.
rule.rationale: All listed tools support `[tool.*]` — splitting config across files is now legacy.

rule.statement: The following files are forbidden in any directory containing a pyproject.toml: setup.cfg, .flake8, pytest.ini, tox.ini, mypy.ini, .coveragerc, requirements.txt, requirements-dev.txt.
rule.rationale: Each legacy file is a separate parser the agent must read; redundancy creates drift.

rule.statement: Runtime deps MUST live in `[project].dependencies`; dev deps in `[project.optional-dependencies].dev`; ad-hoc `pip install` lists in `requirements*.txt` are forbidden on new code.
rule.rationale: A single source of truth for dependencies prevents the "which file is current" question.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `check-no-legacy.sh` (or equivalent) that fails the build if any forbidden legacy file exists.
rule.rationale: Without the check, legacy files re-appear during merges.

rule.statement: `[build-system]` MUST declare a PEP 517 backend (hatchling, setuptools>=64, poetry-core, pdm-backend). `setup.py` MUST NOT carry imperative build logic on new projects.
rule.rationale: PEP 517 backends are the standard; imperative setup.py is legacy and not parseable.

rule.statement: If project requires imperative `setup.py` for native extensions that TOML cannot express OR is a vendored mirror, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when TOML cannot express the build.
